subject: Re: M not responding
body:
Hi everyone,

there is nothing at the weight precalculation step that could take hours. 
Even in the worst case scenario of thousands of particles and super fine 
grid resolution, it should be quicker. So Alister's explanation seems more 
likely. If true, it would be a filesystem issue unrelated to M. Although 
our file servers also run Linux, I've never experienced it.

Generally, the weight precalculation step can crash if some particles' 
positions in the tilt images are outside those images. I added some code to 
exclude individual particle tilts from refinement if that happens in this 
updated version: http://deployment.warpem.com/nightly/Warp_20200802.zip 
(copy and replace files in installation directory).

Side note: I'll be adding experimental versions that I've been sharing with 
some users to test bug fixes as nightly builds under 
http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=65 from now on. They definitely won't 
be updated every night, but I can at least make an effort to upload them in 
the late hours :-)

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Tuesday, August 18, 2020 at 10:31:46 PM UTC+2, Xiao Fan wrote:
>
> Hi Shyamal,
>
> I got a similar problem and I am also searching for solution. It seems 
> you've already refined some TS successfully but I've get stuck at the 
> beginning. I found that the Warpwokers will exit when this happen, but the 
> UI is still running. I'm wondering is there any difference between TS_749 
> and the others? BTW, if I uncheck the "Image warp grid" and "Volume warp 
> grid" option, there's no problem with the refinement.
>
> Best,
> Xiao
>
> 在 2020年8月18日星期二 UTC-4上午11:41:53，shyamal mosalaganti写道：
>>
>> Hi Dimitry and others,
>>
>> I am running M (v1.0.9) and there was no problem. However, after sometime 
>> there is no progress and it stuck on a tilt series (TS_749, please see 
>> attached) for almost an hour now. 
>> Did anyone have a similar problem? and is there a way to fix this?
>> Please let me know,
>> Thank you,
>> Shyamal
>> [image: Screenshot 2020-08-18 at 17.34.59.png]
>>
>
